excluded_defs.name: Match
excluded_defs.reason: read: Router.Match() outer loop — not modified; no special ErrHostMismatch handling needed here unlike ErrMethodMismatch

exploration_log.aha_moment: The host regexp is added to r.matchers via addMatcher, just like method matchers, so it can be intercepted in the same matchers loop using a type assertion to *routeRegexp and checking regexpType == regexpTypeHost.
exploration_log.hindsight: The fix is clean and minimal; no changes needed in Router.Match() since there's no special handler for host mismatches (unlike method mismatches which have MethodNotAllowedHandler).

alternatives: use continue and allow subsequent matchers to override matchErr
key_decisions.decision: Use continue (not return false) when host mismatch is detected
key_decisions.reasoning: Mirrors the ErrMethodMismatch pattern; using continue allows the matchErr to be set and checked at the end of the loop, and allows method failures to take priority (overwriting ErrHostMismatch with ErrMethodMismatch)

solve_notes: Added ErrHostMismatch sentinel variable to mux.go alongside the existing ErrMethodMismatch and ErrNotFound sentinels. In route.go's Route.Match() matchers loop, added a check after the methodMatcher case: when a *routeRegexp with regexpTypeHost fails to match, set matchErr = ErrHostMismatch and continue (same pattern as method mismatch). This allows the MatchErr to propagate up to the caller when host is the only failing constraint.
task_text:
Fix Router.Match not populating RouteMatch.MatchErr for host mismatch

When a request matches a route's path and method but fails the host
constraint (set via `Route.Host()`), `Router.Match()` returns `false`
with `match.MatchErr` left at its zero value instead of being set to
a distinguishable error. This makes it impossible for callers to
tell whether the match failed due to host, path, or method. Fix the
match loop in `mux.go` to set a `ErrHostMismatch` sentinel on
`RouteMatch.MatchErr` when the host matcher is the only failing
constraint.

tests combined host+path routing with mismatching hosts, exercising the matchers loop where host mismatch detection occurs

thrash_preventing_defs.name: regexpTypeHost
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: needed to know the constant value to check against rr.regexpType

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set covers what was directly changed. The thrash_preventing set adds ErrMethodMismatch (so an agent understands the existing pattern to mirror), addRegexpMatcher (so an agent knows the host regexp IS in r.matchers and can be type-asserted), and regexpTypeHost (so an agent knows the exact constant to check). Without these, an agent might search for how host matching works, whether host is in matchers at all, and what constant to use — causing multiple extra read operations.
